summary: M15.4: fused RMSNorm+paro_rotate2 verifier kernel is bit-exact but occupancy-limited (neutral); default-off. The RMSNorm one-block-per-row reduction serializes the per-group rotate, canceling the -30 launch saving.

change.kernel: paro_rmsnorm_rotate2_fp16 (fused input RMSNorm + qkv/z rotate, also writes unrotated RMSNorm to attn_input)
change.bit_exact_test: tests/test_paro_rmsnorm_rotate2.py: tokens {1,3,4,6,8} x hidden {512,1024,2048,4096}, out_norm+out0+out1 byte-identical to rmsnorm->rotate2
change.wired: run_linear_attention_moe_chain_tloop_layer_fp16 (chain verifier; tree path is a task #35 follow-up)

rocprof_b3.note: -30 launches but +4.3% kernel time: the fused kernel runs the per-group rotate in 16 sequential batches (256 threads / 64-lane groups) vs the standalone rotate2 256 parallel blocks.

economics_b3_verify_ms_per_cycle.verdict: noise-dominated, roughly neutral across two back-to-back sessions (s1 ON -6% min, s2 ON +0.8% min/+5.1% mean).

decision_reason: Bit-exact (RED test, incl. production hidden=4096) and exact-AR smoke passes, but the fusion is occupancy-limited: the RMSNorm full-row reduction forces one block per row, serializing the per-group butterfly the standalone rotate runs in parallel. -30 launches is canceled by +4.3% kernel time; economics wall is noise-neutral. Same class of trap as the M13.B consumer-side rotate fusions. Default-off; kept as correct infra + reproducible finding.
notes: Rotate launches (190/pass) resist exact fusion: consumer-side (M13.B.1/B.2) hit a redundancy trap, producer-side (M15.4) hits an occupancy trap.
To beat AR via launch reduction likely needs a fundamentally lower launch count (megakernel / C-side per-layer dispatch), not pairwise op fusion.
